title: Avoid Duplication of String Literals
description: Duplicated string literals complicate the refactoring process and increase the likelihood of errors, as any change needs to be replicated across all occurrences. To minimize false positives, string literals with fewer than 5 characters are excluded.
recommendation: Always use constants for string literals that are reused. This ensures that changes only need to be made in a single place, reducing the potential for errors.
impact: Improves code maintainability by avoiding the need to propagate changes across multiple occurrences of the same string literal.

checks.message: Use constants for reused string literals to improve maintainability
